channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro says the US is fabricating a war as aircraft carrier approaches
item.description: CARACAS (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro said the United States government is forging a war against him as the world's biggest warship approaches the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa nears hurricane strength and dumps torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa has neared hurricane strength, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall, flooding and landslides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? Mystery man goes viral after Louvre heist
item.description: If the unidentified man really is one of the more than 100 investigators hunting for the jewel thieves, the authorities are keeping it very hush-hush.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Object that fell from sky in Australia is likely Chinese rocket debris
item.description: Officials from the Western Australia Police Force said miners in the Pilbara region reported discovering the blackened, smoldering mass Oct. 18.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Frantic manhunt launched after asylum seeker who sexually assaulted teen accidentally freed from prison
item.description: An Ethiopian asylum seeker who sexually assaulted 14-year-old girl was accidentally released from a U.K. prison instead of being deported, sparking an urgent manhunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: US kills 6 suspected narco-terrorists in overnight strike on alleged drug smuggling boat, Hegseth says
item.description: War Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the tenth strike on an alleged drug smuggling boat, saying another six alleged narco-terrorists were killed overnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkish court dismisses case challenging main opposition party's congress
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A Turkish court on Friday dismissed a case challenging the legitimacy of the main opposition party’s 2023 internal election, ruling that there was no legal basis to remove the current leadership from office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO fighter jets scrambled after Russian planes allegedly violate airspace
item.description: Spanish NATO jets were scrambled after two Russian aircraft reportedly violated Lithuanian airspace, escalating tensions amid the ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey in talks with Qatar and Oman to buy used Eurofighter jets, Erdogan says
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey is negotiating with Qatar and Oman to acquire used Eurofighter Typhoon fighter jets as part of its effort to bolster its air force capabilities, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said in comments released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: US strikes two more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Louvre director grilled on spectacular security failures, including camera pointing away from key balcony
item.description: Louvre Museum director Laurence des Cars acknowledged that security failures, including a lack of perimeter surveillance, allowed thieves to pull off heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth says US conducted another strike in Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-traffickers
item.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday that the U.S. military has carried out another strike in the Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top UN court says Israel must allow UN relief agency to supply aid to Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Court of Justice said on Wednesday that Israel must allow the U.N. aid agency in Gaza, known as UNRWA, to provide humanitarian assistance to the Palestinian territory. The Hague-based court was asked late last year by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Expert warns critical hours slipping away as kidnappers likely to move US missionary in Niger
item.description: American missionary kidnapped near Niger's presidential palace as security expert warns first 48 hours critical for rescue from ISIS-controlled territory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dublin protesters clash with police, burn vehicle after migrant accused of sexually assaulting Irish girl
item.description: Dublin, Ireland, protests turned violent Tuesday and a police car was set ablaze after a migrant allegedly sexually assaulted a girl in state child care.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance is optimistic about Gaza ceasefire but notes 'very hard' work to come
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday called progress in Gaza’s fragile ceasefire better than anticipated but acknowledged during an Israel visit the challenges that remain, from disarming Hamas to rebuilding a land devastated by two years of war.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO, MELANIE LIDMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel identifies 2 hostages returned from Gaza as residents of same kibbutz
item.description: Israel identified the two hostages returned from Gaza through the Red Cross as an 85-year-old man taken by Hamas and an IDF reserve master sergeant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance warns Hamas as Gaza peace plan's Civilian Military Cooperation Center opens
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said that he's confident in "durable" peace amid the ceasefire, warning Hamas will be "obliterated" if it doesn't cooperate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: Burqa ban: Portugal may make it illegal to cover face in public
item.description: If signed into law, Portugal would join Austria, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, who have full or partial bans on face and head coverings.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation
